zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Davenport於1525年出生在英國爾布拉哈爾時，她的父親William Davenport II53歲，母親Blanche Warburton29歲。她於1541年與Sir Robert Hyde在英國結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1590年8月23日在英國英格蘭爾布拉哈爾享年65歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Davenport于1525年出生在英国爾布拉哈爾时，她的父亲William Davenport II53岁，母亲Blanche Warburton29岁。她于1541年与Sir Robert Hyde在英国结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1590年8月23日在英国英格兰爾布拉哈爾享年65岁，埋葬在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Davenport
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): habitational name from a place in Cheshire named Davenport, from the Dane river (apparently named with a Celtic cognate of Middle Welsh dafnu ‘to drop, to trickle’) + Old English port ‘market town’.

Irish: in Tipperary, this is an English surname adopted by bearers of Munster Gaelic Ó Donndubhartaigh ‘descendant of Donndubhartach’, a personal name composed of the elements donn ‘brown-haired man’ or ‘chieftain’ + dubh ‘black’ + artach ‘nobleman’.
History: John Davenport (died 1670) arrived in Boston, MA, in 1637. He came of an English Cheshire family associated with Capesthorne Hall, near Macclesfield.

variants.name: Devenport
meanings: English and Irish (Lancashire): variant of Davenport . The spelling Devonport is found in Ireland and it may have been this form which gave rise to Devenport.

variants.name: Dunworth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Eamon, Niall.
Irish: adopted in Ireland by bearers of Gaelic Ó Donndubhartaigh (see Davenport 2).
English: perhaps a variant of Dungworth, see Dunsworth .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Davenport was born in 1525, in Bramhall, Cheshire, England, her father, William Davenport II, was 53 and her mother, Blanche Warburton, was 29. She married Sir Robert Hyde in 1541, in England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 7 daughters. She died on 23 August 1590, in Bramhall, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 65, and was buried in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Davenport est née en 1525, à Bramhall, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, William Davenport II, avait 53 ans et sa mère, Blanche Warburton, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Robert Hyde en 1541, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée le 23 août 1590, à Bramhall, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterrée à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Davenport 1525 in Bramhall, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Davenport II 53 und ihre Mutter Blanche Warburton 29. Sie hat 1541 in England Sir Robert Hyde geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb am 23. August 1590 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Bramhall, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Davenport è nata il nel 1525 nel Bramhall, Cheshire, England, sua padre, William Davenport II, aveva 53 anni e sua madre, Blanche Warburton, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato Sir Robert Hyde nel 1541, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 23 agosto 1590, nel Bramhall, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolta a Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Davenportが1525年にイギリスチェシュアイアブラムハルで生まれた時，父William Davenport IIは53歳，母Blanche Warburtonは29歳であった。1541年にイギリスでSir Robert Hydeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1590年8月23日にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアブラムハルで65歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Davenport(이)가 1525년 영국 체실르 브램홀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Davenport II(은)는 53세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Blanche Warburton(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1541년 영국에서 Sir Robert Hyde(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1590년 8월 23일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 브램홀에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Davenport nasceu em 1525, em Bramhall, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Davenport II, tinha 53 anos e sua mãe, Blanche Warburton, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Robert Hyde em 1541, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela faleceu em 23 de agosto de 1590, em Bramhall, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 65 anos, e foi sepultada em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Davenport родилась в 1525 г. в местности брамхолл, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени William Davenport II было 53 года, а матери, которую звали Blanche Warburton, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Robert Hyde в 1541 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Она умерла 23 августа 1590 г. в местности брамхолл, чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 65 лет и была похоронена в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Davenport nació en 1525, en Bramhall, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Davenport II, tenía 53 años y su mamá, Blanche Warburton, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Sir Robert Hyde en 1541, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 23 de agosto de 1590, en Bramhall, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultada en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
